title: Threat Models, Untrusted Content, XSS, Paths, Templates, and CSP · Runtime Lens: Template Path Defense
summary: Raw HTML executes author input, javascript links survive, a template loader escapes its root, copied SVG contains active content, secrets enter output, and CSP is treated as the only XSS defense. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the template path defense increment in explicit static-publishing trust and security policy. Learners progress through…
objectives: Constrain content, include, template, output, and asset paths; reject traversal, symlink escape, special files, and output overlap.
Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Template Path Defense · Theory Template Path Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 7354 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Template Path Defense contract for the supplied data.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-template-path-defense — Constrain content, include, template, output, and asset paths; reject traversal, symlink escape, special files, and output overlap.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-security-trust-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-security-trust-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_security_trust_2(untrusted_cases: int = 12, rejected_or_encoded: int = 12, path_escapes: int = 0, secret_findings: int = 0, unsafe_urls: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if untrusted_cases < 1 or rejected_or_encoded < 0:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "threat-cases"
    if rejected_or_encoded != untrusted_cases:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "injection-coverage"
    if path_escapes > 0 or secret_findings > 0:
        return False, path_escapes + secret_findings, "publication-leak"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    assert rejected_or_encoded == untrusted_cases
    return True, rejected_or_encoded, "bounded-trust-policy"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-template-path-defense-1gpah6
# Competency: ssg-template-path-defense.
# Case 2980: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_template_path_defense_1gpah6(untrusted_cases: int = 12, rejected_or_encoded: int = 12, path_escapes: int = 0, secret_findings: int = 0, unsafe_urls: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1gpah6 = "1gpah6-6hapg1-2980"
    assert evidence_variant_1gpah6
    evidence_axes_1gpah6 = (
        "reader1gpah6",
        "source2980",
        "parse6hapg1",
        "route1gpah62980",
        "artifact29801gpah6",
        "recovery6hapg12980",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1gpah6)) == 6
    if untrusted_cases < 1 or rejected_or_encoded < 0:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "threat-cases"
    if rejected_or_encoded != untrusted_cases:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "injection-coverage"
    if path_escapes > 0 or secret_findings > 0:
        return False, path_escapes + secret_findings, "publication-leak"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    assert rejected_or_encoded == untrusted_cases
    return True, rejected_or_encoded, "bounded-trust-policy"
